statement.en: Mapping emotions in bad religion’s music : insights from qualitative and automatic sentiment analysis
statement.summary: In this chapter, we analysed Bad Religion’s seventeen studio albums’ emotional expressions using automatic sentiment analysis to map the emotional trends over time, and to evaluate how these emotions are manifested. The automatic sentiment analysis revealed prevailing negative or neutral tones, depending on the model used (i.e. the Ekman model and the Go Emotions model). The most prominent emotions also vary, from fear, sadness, and anger, on the one side, and annoyance, and disappointment, on the other. Despite slight variations across the albums, no distinct upward or downward trends were identified for specific emotions. The qualitative analysis disclosed that most emotion-related terms occur infrequently (one - nine occurrences) in the lyrics. We assessed the use of emotion words in ex

reiTyping.dfumt8Reason: This is an empirical study (2026, preprint) applying established sentiment analysis methods to a specific cultural corpus. The findings are provisionally valid pending peer review and validation against independent datasets. The methodological approach is sound but results are contingent on model choice (Ekman vs. Go Emotions) and corpus-specific constraints.
